2606.29914.title: MemDelta: Controlled Baselines and Hidden Confounds in Agent Memory Evaluation
2606.29914.paper_summary: Agent memory systems are increasingly evaluated against RAG and full-context baselines, but reported gains often mix changes in the memory method with changes in the language model, embedding model, or retrieval pipeline, making it unclear what is actually being measured. We present MemDelta, a controlled evaluation protocol that varies one component at a time on LongMemEval-S (500 questions, 50+ sessions, three model families). Four findings emerge: (1) verbatim RAG matches full-context GPT-4o-mini (47.2% vs. 49.8%, p = 0.34), but the ranking reverses across models: Gemini gains +14pp from full context, while Sonnet gains +31pp from RAG, partly because it refuses 63% of full-context queries; (2) swapping only the embedding model in an identical pipeline shifts accuracy by +6.2pp at n = 500 (p = 0.004), and Mem0 beats MiniLM-RAG by +11pp but loses to cloud-RAG by 1.2pp, so one variable flips the conclusion; (3) agent self-memory (42%) underperforms basic retrieval (47%); (4) on 2 of 6 question types (n = 88), Mem0 matches cloud RAG (72.7% vs. 73.9%, p = 1.0) at 50x the cost, suggesting narrow rather than general gains. We recommend memory evaluations fix embedding models across comparisons, stratify by model family, and report write-path cost before attributing gains to architecture.
2606.29914.paper_summary_zh: 代理記憶系統越來越多地與 RAG 和全上下文基準進行評估，但報告的增益往往將記憶方法的變化與語言模型、嵌入模型或檢索管道的變化混合在一起，這使得實際測量的內容變得不清楚。我們提出了 MemDelta，一種控制評估協議，該協議在 LongMemEval-S（500 個問題，50+ 次會話，三個模型系列）上一次變化一個組件。四個發現浮現出來：（1）逐字 RAG 與全上下文 GPT-4o-mini 匹配（47.2% 對 49.8%，p = 0.34），但模型之間的排名反轉：Gemini 從全上下文中獲得 +14pp，而 Sonnet 從 RAG 中獲得 +31pp，部分原因是它拒絕了 63% 的全上下文查詢；（2）在相同管道中僅更換嵌入模型會使準確率提高 +6.2pp，n = 500（p = 0.004），而 Mem0 比 MiniLM-RAG 高出 +11pp，但輸給 cloud-RAG 1.2pp，因此一個變量翻轉了結論；（3）代理自我記憶（42%）表現不如基本檢索（47%）；（4）在 6 種問題類型中的 2 種（n = 88）中，Mem0 與 cloud RAG 匹配（72.7% 對 73.9%，p = 1.0），但成本是其 50 倍，這表明增益是狹窄的而非一般性的。我們建議記憶評估在比較中固定嵌入模型，按模型系列分層，並在將增益歸因於架構之前報告寫路徑成本。
2606.29914.author: Kuan Wang et.al.
